growth_records.medium: WCA (Wilkins-Chalgren Anaerobe broth, Oxoid CM0643) with supplemental mucin (Sigma M1778, type III)

up.method: better growth with mucin; mucin-CAZyme genes upregulated by RNA-seq

growth_records.cite: Ducarmon et al. Cayman enables large-scale analysis of gut microbiome carbohydrate-active enzyme repertoires. Nat Microbiol (2026). DOI:10.1038/s41564-026-02318-2. [PMC13236585]
growth_records.snippet: We observed better growth (faster outgrowth, increased growth rates and/or higher yields) in the presence of mucin for all three species ... E. tayi and H. hathewayi, the two species that grew to higher yields in WCA
growth_records.notes: OD578 growth curve only (Fig.3b); no numeric growth rate/doubling time/flux reported (figure-only). Grew to higher yield in WCA with mucin. Commercial WCA broth composition not stated.

phenotypes.base_medium: Enzyme / activity assay (hydrolysis, degradation, reduction, electron transfer) in a test medium — not a sole-substrate growth test
phenotypes.citation: BacDive strain 2808 (Reimer LC et al., BacDive 2022, NAR); https://bacdive.dsmz.de/strain/2808

phenotypes.base_medium: Sole-substrate growth / assimilation on a defined minimal medium (substrate as C/N/energy source) — positive = the organism grows on it
